text.title: Kubernetes LimitRange Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules plus a skip-clause for `Kubernetes LimitRange`. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every LimitRange MUST set both default (limit) AND defaultRequest for CPU + memory at the Container level.
rule.rationale: Without defaults pods admitted with no requests get scheduled arbitrarily; without default limits noisy pods OOM neighbours.

rule.statement: Production namespaces MUST set max for CPU + memory at the Container level.
rule.rationale: Without max a single pod can request the whole node + starve everything.

rule.statement: Production namespaces SHOULD set maxLimitRequestRatio (e.g. cpu 10, memory 4) to bound burst.
rule.rationale: Without ratio, a 100m-request pod can declare 10-CPU limit and starve scheduling.

rule.statement: defaultRequest MUST be greater than 0 for both CPU + memory; zero defaults are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Zero requests get arbitrary scheduling; the QoS class collapses to BestEffort.

rule.statement: Each environment-tier (dev/staging/prod) MUST have its own LimitRange profile; one-size-fits-all is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Dev needs permissive limits for spikes; prod needs strict ratio enforcement.

rule.statement: If Applies If preconditions fail OR any Skip If trigger fires, do not run this methodology.
